original: Plans to build Disneyland 1st announced [see Jan 26]
New study suggests the asteroid that wiped out the dinosaurs 66 million years ago created South America's tropical rainforests
17-year old girl Jackie Mitchell strikes out New York Yankees starsBabe RuthandLou Gehrigin an exhibition baseball game at Engel Stadium in Chattanooga, Tennessee
Albert Einsteinlectures in New York City on his new "Theory of Relativity"
1st human cannonball act performed by 14-year-old Rossa Matilda Richter known as Zazel at the Royal Aquarium in London
Wind speed reaches a record 450 kph in tornado, Wichita Falls, Texas
Footage of deepest living fish ever recorded, the snailfish, released caught on camera at depth of 27,349 feet (8,336 meters) in the Izu-Ogasawara Trench, south of Japan
"Tarzanthe Ape Man" released starring Olympic swimmerJohnny Weissmullerin the first of his 12 Tarzan films
"2001A Space Odyssey" directed byStanley Kubrickand starring Keir Dullea and Gary Lockwood, premieres at the Uptown Theater in Washington, D.C.
Soviet Union's Luna 10 becomes 1st spacecraft to orbit Moon
Scottish physicistRobert Watson-Wattreceives a British patents for RADAR
New study shows western Antarctica once swampy with temperate forests 93-83 million years ago during Cretaceous period, according to Alfred Wegener Institute
Lake Tulare, once the largest body of water west of the Mississippi, re-emerges after a series of storms in California's Central Valley to cover 160 square miles
NASA states it wants to send astronauts to Mars by 2033, and land on the Moon again in 2024
Bubba Stewart becomes first African-American to win a major motor sports event when he takes out the Monster Energy AMA Supercross C'ship event in Irving, Texas
Georges de Mestral's patent for "velvet type fabric" expires, puts "Velcro" in the open market
First official panda crossing opens outside Waterloo Station in London
In his first full season in the NHL,Wayne Gretzkyscores for Edmonton in a 1-1 tie with Minnesota North Stars to become the youngest player to reach 50 goals at 19 years and 2 months of age
Brian Goodell, American swimmer who won Olympic gold 400m WR 3:51.93, 1500m WR 15:02.40 1976, born in Stockton, California
H L Fizeau & Leon Foucault take 1st photo of the Sun
RMS Titanicundergoes sea trials under its own power
Canada is warming at twice the speed of the rest of the world, according to a federal report, increasing 2.3% in northern Canada
1st Easter egg roll held on White House lawn
Mabry Harper catches a world record 25lb Walleye at Cedar Bluff on the Cumberland River in Tennessee
Lord Skelmersdale [Roger Bootle-Wilbraham], British politician and horticulturalist who created Britain's first toad tunnel, born in England (d. 2018)
USS Hornet with Jimmy Doolittles B-25 departs from San Francisco
Electric Theatre, the first full-time movie theater in the United States, opens in Los Angeles, California
US engineer George Brayton patents an internal combustion engine (Brayton Cycle)
Jack Brabham, Australian auto racer and team owner (World F1 champion 1959, 60, 66 [only man to win F1 C'ship driving one of his own cars]), born in Sydney, Australia (d. 2014)
Ferenc Puskás, Hungarian soccer forward (Hungary 85 caps, Spain 4; Real Madrid, Budapest Honvéd) and manager (Panathinaikos), born in Budapest, Hungary (d. 2006)
Mike Hailwood, British motorcycle road racer (World C'ships: 250cc – 1961, 1966, 1967; 350cc – 1966, 1967; 500cc –1962-65; 76 Grand Prix wins), born in Great Milton, England (d. 1981)
New York Islanders right wingMike Bossyscores in a 6-3 win over the Pittsburgh Penguins to become the first player in NHL history to score 60 goals in 3 consecutive seasons
Calgary Flames right wing Joe Mullen sets new NHL record for most points in a season by a American-born player, with a goal and 2 assists in a 4-2 win over Edmonton Oilers gives him 110 points
OKC guard Russell Westbrook becomes 2nd player in NBA history to have 20+ points, rebounds and assists in a game; records 20-20-21 in 119-103 win over LA Lakers
ICC Men's Cricket ODI World Cup, Wankhede Stadium, Mumbai, India: India beats Sri Lanka by 6 wickets for their second title; Player of the Match:MS Dhoni(IND) 91 no (79)
Vancouver Grizzlies beat Minnesota Timberwolves, 105-103 at General Motors Place Arena to end their NBA single-season record 23-game consecutive loss streak
Sri Lankan cricket batsmanSanath Jayasuriyahits century in 48 balls (134 off 65), in 34 run win over Pakistan in Singapore; world ODI record
Seattle outfielderIchiro Suzukihas 2 hits and becomes first Japanese position player to play in a regular season MLB game as Mariners beat Oakland A's, 5-4 at Safeco Field
At 27 years, 249 days Texas Rangers infielderAlex Rodriguezbecomes the youngest MLB player to hit 300 home runs; homers off Ramon Ortiz in 5th inning of an 11-5 loss to the Angels in Anaheim

Longest strike in American major league sports history (232 days) ends as MLB owners accept players’ offer to return to work without a contract; MLB is the first major pro sports league to lose an entire postseason due to a labor dispute
Cleveland Cavaliers guard Mark Price has his consecutive free throw streak end at 77, during a 114-113 loss at Charlotte; falls 1 short of Calvin Murphy’s NBA record of 78 straight free throws

Adam Gilchrist takes his 10th catch in the 3rd cricket Test against New Zealand in Hamilton to create an Australian record for dismissals by a wicketkeeper; Australia wins by 6 wickets for 3-0 series sweep
Linford Christie, British athlete (Olympic gold men's 100m 1992), born in Saint Andrew, Jamaica
Space Shuttle STS-45 (Atlantis 11) lands
"Eye of the Needle", a revolving restaurant, opens atop the Space Needle at the Century 21 Exposition (World's Fair) in Seattle, Washington; designed by architect John Graham, Jr
Sixth Impressionist Exhibition opens in Paris organized byEdgar Degasand showing his famous "Little Dancer of Fourteen Years", the only sculpture shown in his lifetime
Powerful spring storms begin crossing the US Midwest and mid-South, killing at least 23 people in Kentucky, Arkansas, Tennessee and Missouri
Des Bartlett [Norman Desmond Bartlett], Australian wildlife photographer and documentarian (The Flight of the Snow Geese, Survivors of the Skeleton Coast), born in Canungra, Australia (d. 2009)
Max Ernst, German-French surrealist painter and sculptor (founded surrealism), born in Brühl, German Empire (d. 1976)
Nicolay G. Basov, Soviet physicist who specialized in quantum electronics (laser, maser) and 1964 Nobel laureate, born in Usman, Russia (d. 2001)
Leon Russell [Claude Russell Bridges], American musician and singer-songwriter ("Tight Rope"; "Delta Lady"; "Carney"), born in Lawton, Oklahoma (d. 2016)
Hiroyuki Sakai, Japanese chef who specializes in French cuisine (Iron Chef), born in Izumi, Kagoshima, Japan
Charles "Honi" Coles, American actor and tap dancer (Tito Suarez-Dirty Dancing), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 1992)
Herbert Mills, American jazz and pop singer and vocal trumpet (The Mills Brothers - "Paper Doll", "Up A Lazy River", "The Glow-Worm"), born in Piqua, Ohio (d. 1989)
Charles White, American artist (The Contribution of the Negro to American Democracy), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 1979)
Mark Shulman, American children's author (Scrawl), born in Rochester, New York
Garnett Silk [Garnet Smith], Jamaican reggae singer and songwriter (Hey Mama Africa; Greenline), born in Manchester, Jamaica (d. 1994)
Ayako Okamoto, Japanese golfer (US Open 1987 runner-up, 17 LPGA Tour titles), born in Hiroshima, Japan
Gelindo Bordin, Italian athlete (Olympic gold marathon 1988), born in Vicenza, Italy
Joan D. Vinge, American sci-fi author (2 Hugos, Dune), born in Baltimore, Maryland
